spec_paraphrase: Stage 3 minimises activation-aware Frobenius reconstruction at rank k via the AA-SVD/Swift-SVD pipeline (§6).
paper_verbatim: W*_k = arg min_{Wk in k} ||XW - XWk||_F
notes: Objective implicitly adopted; not a deviation.

spec_paraphrase: FactoredExperts stores U_k and V_k^T factors (Phase C).
notes: Standard low-rank storage.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not exploit XAk caching for KV reduction (this paper claim is specific to attention KV; pipeline targets MoE FFN experts).
paper_verbatim: instead of caching output activations XW... one can cache intermediate latents X Ak
notes: Out of scope: spec compresses MoE expert MLPs, not attention KV cache.

spec_paraphrase: Spec quotes 'optimal low-rank approximation of every layer is computed using the closed-form solution in (3)' but actually factors with AA-SVD Theorem 3.2 (paper 2604.02119), not Swift-SVD's Eq. 3 = W·V_k·V_k^T.
notes: Paraphrase drift: spec attributes its α-search factoring to Swift-SVD Eq. 3, but the actual factoring path is Phase C AA-SVD (M = W·C·B^{-1}·L_B). Both target activation-aware reconstruction; not a numerical deviation but the citation is misattributed.

spec_paraphrase: Spec cites Eq. 3 verbatim in the α-search description, but factoring is performed via AA-SVD Theorem 3.2 (different formula).
notes: Same as #thm3.1: misattribution of the closed-form.

spec_paraphrase: ε*_i = sqrt(Σ_{j>k̄} σ̃_j² / Σ_j σ̃_j²) — normalized tail-energy of singular values of W·A^{1/2}.
notes: Spec normalizes by total spectral energy (relative tail energy) instead of paper's absolute L2 tail; D8 mentions ε* is now activation-weighted (W·A^{1/2}) which matches paper's Y=XW spirit. Normalization difference is minor but undocumented.

spec_paraphrase: Proof step; not implemented in spec.
notes: Theorem proof; no implementation requirement.

spec_paraphrase: Optimality bound; proof step.
notes: Proof artifact.

spec_paraphrase: Rank upper bound; proof step.
notes: Proof artifact.

spec_paraphrase: Rank lower bound; proof step.
notes: Proof artifact.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 2 streams activations and accumulates A = X^T X covariance (FP16 storage); Stage 3 reuses it. The B-cov collection in Phase A also follows the streaming covariance + eigendecomposition pattern.
paper_verbatim: C ← C + yt^T yt; V, Sigma^2, V^T ← eigen-decomposition(C)
notes: Spec follows streaming-covariance + single eigh pattern.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses eigendecomposition of covariance to recover spectral basis (Phase C eigh caching).
notes: Identical algebra.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses D-Rank's spectral entropy formula (paper 2509.25622) for rank budget, not Swift-SVD's effective-rank metric.
paper_verbatim: erank(Sigma) = exp(- sum p_i ln p_i)
notes: Out of scope for Swift-SVD's role in spec; D-Rank already covers entropy-based allocation. Not flagged.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not invoke the negative-correlation insight to motivate score si — uses spectral-energy β instead of layer-importance β.
paper_verbatim: local compressibility ... and end-to-end compressibility ... are negatively correlated
notes: Motivational claim; spec's β substitution (D8) breaks the link to this insight.

spec_paraphrase: Spec replaces ShortGPT-style β with per-expert spectral energy share; no layer-importance forward passes are run.
paper_verbatim: we adopt the standard layer importance metric from prior work ... lower layer importance implies higher end-to-end compressibility
notes: D8 explicitly documents this β substitution and justifies it by the per-expert (vs per-layer) granularity adaptation.

spec_paraphrase: Algorithm 2 is applied per-(layer,matrix_type,expert) group rather than per-layer; β replaced (D8); δ floor and explicit b = k̄·L − Σk_i pool arithmetic not described in spec; total budget per group is k_g × N_experts (D-Rank's k_g, not Swift-SVD's k̄ = m·n/(m+n)·ρ).
paper_verbatim: Input: ... beta, Compression ratio rho, Scaling factor alpha, Retained ratio delta, Size m,n. ... 1: beta ← (beta - min(beta))/(max(beta) - min(beta)) + 1   2: k_bar = (m*n)/(m+n)*rho   ...
notes: Major adaptation: paper indexes over L layers; spec indexes over experts within a (layer, matrix_type) group. D8 covers the β change and granularity shift in its justification, but does not call out (a) absence of δ floor, (b) k̄ replaced by k_g, (c) min-max+1 normalization absence, or (d) flexible-pool arithmetic. See findings F-paper-2604.01609-0001..0004.

spec_paraphrase: s_i = β_i^α · (log(e + ε*_i))^{1-α} — formula reproduced verbatim.
notes: Score formula structurally identical; β and ε* semantics differ (D8).

spec_paraphrase: Spec defines β_i = σ_i² / Σ σ_j² (already in [0,1] per group, sums to 1) — no min-max+1 shift to [1,2] is applied.
notes: Without the +1 shift, β_i^α can collapse to ~0 for small spectral shares, distorting the score relative to the paper's intended [1,2^α] dynamic range. Not covered by D8 (D8 only addresses the β SOURCE, not the normalization). See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0001.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses D-Rank's k_g (group rank from spectral-entropy allocation, paper 2509.25622) instead of Swift-SVD's k̄ = m·n/(m+n)·ρ. The 'group total budget' for redistribution is k_g × N_experts.
notes: Substitution is internally consistent (D-Rank provides the group budget; Swift-SVD redistributes within it) but the spec does not explicitly call this out as a deviation from Swift-SVD's k̄ definition. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0002.

spec_paraphrase: Spec's Phase B.2 description does not mention any guaranteed minimal rank floor k_i ← k̄·δ; it only says ranks are 'redistributed' across experts.
paper_verbatim: ki ← k_bar * delta   ▷ Guaranteed minimal rank
notes: Critical paper safeguard absent from spec; without δ floor, redistribution can over-compress experts with low spectral share — exactly the failure mode Swift-SVD(I)/(C) ablation flagged. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0003.

spec_paraphrase: Spec describes redistribution as 'redistributing the group's total rank budget k_g × N_experts across individual experts' but does not explicitly state b = k̄·L − Σ k_i floor accounting.
notes: Without δ floor (see #minimal_rank), the flexible-pool arithmetic collapses into 'distribute k_g·N_experts proportionally to s_i' — equivalent to setting δ=0, which the paper explicitly warns is unstable. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0004.

spec_paraphrase: Per-expert ranks are allocated proportional to s_i within the group; arithmetic implicit (no δ floor, no explicit pool b).
notes: Granularity adapted (per-expert vs per-layer) — this part is sanctioned by D8. Arithmetic gap (no δ floor) flagged separately in #minimal_rank/#flex_pool.

spec_paraphrase: α ∈ {0.0, 0.1, ..., 1.0} — 11 candidates (matches paper exactly).
notes: α grid matches; δ=0.5 NOT mentioned in spec (see #delta_role).

spec_paraphrase: End-to-end PPL validation on WikiText-2 (512 sequences); α minimizing PPL selected. Paper-compliance contract enforces no spectral-proxy fallback (D9 removed).
paper_verbatim: the optimal low-rank approximation of every layer is computed using the closed-form solution in (3). The resulting compressed models are then evaluated on a validation set, and the candidate that yields the best end-to-end performance is selected
notes: Recent commit (cf786a1) confirmed paper-exact protocol; D9 spectral-proxy removed. Implementation TODO at stage3_svd.py:285-301 still has OOM auto-fallback per spec line 392 — flagged for phase 7 implementation audit, not phase 5 spec audit.

spec_paraphrase: Spec notes 'α balances the two signals' but does not document endpoint semantics.
paper_verbatim: alpha = 0 reduces ... to a purely loss-driven allocation, whereas alpha = 1 prioritizes layer importance exclusively
notes: Pedagogical detail; not load-bearing for implementation.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not implement δ floor at all (see #minimal_rank); paper warns δ=0 is unstable and recommends δ=0.5.
paper_verbatim: delta acts as a performance stabilizer. Without a preserved ratio delta = 0, unrestricted dynamic allocation can inadvertently destroy the representational capacity of critical layers
notes: Spec effectively runs δ=0 — the paper's explicitly-warned-against unstable regime. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0003.

spec_paraphrase: Spec applies per-projection rank multipliers gate=1.33, up=0.67, down=1.0 before per-expert redistribution. D7a's 'Paper Says' field cites 2509.25622 (D-Rank), not 2604.01609 (Swift-SVD).
paper_verbatim: Swift-SVD can be seamlessly applied to all types of weight matrices–such as query, key, value, and others–in the same manner. For simplicity, we use the generic notation W throughout our exposition.
notes: D7a sanctions the per-projection bias against D-Rank but does NOT acknowledge that Swift-SVD also prescribes per-projection uniformity (Algorithm 2 indexes only over layers i ∈ {1..L}, not over projections). The bias is therefore a deviation from BOTH papers; D7a only covers one. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0005. Per the user-supplied playbook hint: 'verify spec §12 D7a row's Paper Says actually represents this absence' — it does not.

spec_paraphrase: Spec splits the rank pool per (layer, matrix_type) (and further per expert). Paper computes per-projection effective rank and ε* but redistributes per-layer only.
paper_verbatim: redistribution in Algorithm 2 is per layer i in {1..L} (not per projection)
notes: Granularity shift related to D7a. D-Rank already partitions per (layer, matrix_type) so this is largely a D-Rank framing; Swift-SVD just operates within each group as the spec describes.

spec_paraphrase: Calibration sample budget set by Stage 2 (multi-domain Nemotron, 1024 samples per spec line 274); not the paper's N=256 from WikiText-2/C4.
paper_verbatim: we adopt the standard setting of N = 256
notes: D11 covers calibration source (Nemotron vs c4 for paper 2603.02217); sample-count delta is incidental and within the paper's diminishing-returns regime.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not specify Stage 3 calibration sequence length explicitly.
notes: Implementation detail; not flagged.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses Stage 2's calibration formatting; no explicit prompt-template handling for Swift-SVD.
paper_verbatim: samples are constructed by formatting individual raw data entries according to their official prompt templates
notes: Implementation detail.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses H200 GPUs; not the paper's RTX 5090.
notes: Hardware substitution; not a numerical deviation.

spec_paraphrase: Spec targets Qwen3-30B MoE; paper targets dense LLaMA/OPT/Mistral/Qwen3 4B/8B.
paper_verbatim: LLaMA-7B, LLaMA2-7B, OPT-6.7B, Mistral-7B, and Qwen3 (4B, 8B)
notes: Model-target choice; out of paper's evaluated set.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses WikiText-2 PPL for the α-search validation metric (subset of paper's metric suite).
notes: PPL is the paper-cited grid-search metric.

spec_paraphrase: Reported numbers; spec does not reproduce LLaMA-7B Table 1.
notes: Reproducibility benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: Reported numbers.
notes: Reproducibility benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: Reported numbers.
notes: Reproducibility benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: Baseline PPL.
notes: Reference.

spec_paraphrase: Cross-model results.
notes: Reproducibility benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: Cross-domain calibration table.
paper_verbatim: Calibration on C4 then evaluation: ratio 0.8 ...
notes: Reproducibility benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: Speedup table.
notes: Reported metric.

spec_paraphrase: Numerical stability table.
notes: Reproducibility benchmark.

spec_paraphrase: Ablation table.
notes: Notable: spec's δ-floor absence (see #minimal_rank/#delta_role) corresponds to the (C)/(I) variants which the ablation shows are WORSE than uniform (Swift-SVD 11.42 vs (C) 16.04, (I) 14.88). Severity boost flagged on #delta_role/#minimal_rank.

spec_paraphrase: Variant taxonomy.
paper_verbatim: Swift-SVD†(C) and Swift-SVD†(I), which incorporate a fixed preserved ratio of delta = 0.5
notes: Definitions only.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses log(e + ε*_i) damping per Eq. 12.
notes: Damping inherited.

spec_paraphrase: Motivational claim; spec uses blended score (Eq. 12) so Frobenius-only bias is avoided by construction.
paper_verbatim: Frobenius loss is disproportionately sensitive to large singular values
notes: Implicitly addressed.

spec_paraphrase: Calibration-size sensitivity claim.
paper_verbatim: performance improves rapidly in the low-sample regime but shows diminishing returns
notes: Spec uses 1024 (Stage 2) which is in the saturated regime.

spec_paraphrase: Cross-domain claim.
paper_verbatim: significant PPL degradation is observed when the calibrated model with C4 is applied to WikiText-2 or Alpaca
notes: Spec calibrates on multi-domain Nemotron (D11) and validates α on WikiText-2; potential domain-shift inflation is expected per this claim but not flagged here.

spec_paraphrase: Headline contributions; framing only.
paper_verbatim: (1) optimal solution ... (2) fast layer-wise compression ... (3) negative correlation
notes: Framing.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses eigh of covariance (FP16 storage on disk, accumulated in higher precision), avoiding Cholesky.
notes: FP16 covariance storage (line 274) may slightly weaken paper's exact-optimum-in-FP32 claim (#fp_precision_table5), but eigh is preserved.

spec_paraphrase: Visual confirmation; no implementation hook.
paper_verbatim: Figure 3 visualizes the normalized effective rank and normalized layer importance
notes: n/a.

spec_paraphrase: Framing.
paper_verbatim: closed-form compression framework that simultaneously guarantees theoretical optimum, practical efficiency and numerical stability
notes: Framing.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 2 covariance is stored in FP16 (line 274 explicit). Paper certifies exact-optimum stability in FP32 only.
paper_verbatim: Reconstruction loss and absolute error for randomly generated matrices of varying shapes under ratio of 0.6 (FP32).
notes: FP16 storage chosen to avoid bf16 precision loss on H200 (line 274 rationale = VRAM/storage). Not flagged in Ch.12. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0006.

spec_paraphrase: U-shape claim; sanity check on grid search necessity.
paper_verbatim: Both configurations exhibit a U-shaped PPL curve
notes: Justifies grid-search necessity already adopted.

spec_paraphrase: Reproducibility check value (LLaMA-7B C4 0.8 → 11.15 PPL).
notes: Spec targets Qwen3-30B; not directly reproducible.

spec_paraphrase: Spec adopts task-aware multi-domain calibration (Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data) consistent with the paper's 'Each ≳ All > C4' hierarchy.
paper_verbatim: Each ≳ All > C4. Domain Specificity (Each): Achieves the highest accuracy
notes: D11 sanctions multi-domain calibration; aligns with this claim.

spec_paraphrase: Spec does not document δ at all (no value, no floor mechanism). See #minimal_rank, #delta_role.
paper_verbatim: Swift-SVD uses a fixed retention ratio delta = 0.5 ... delta acts as a performance stabilizer
notes: Combined with #minimal_rank/#delta_role: spec runs the paper's δ=0 unstable regime. See finding F-paper-2604.01609-0003.
